methodsAttempted.query: site:umontreal.ca OR site:enseigner.umontreal.ca Université de Montréal intelligence artificielle générative ChatGPT enseignement évaluation étudiants politique
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official UdeM teaching, academic-integrity, news, faculty-toolbox, and graduate-studies sources. Search snippets were used only for discovery.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umontreal.ca Université de Montréal IA générative données personnelles confidentialité ChatGPT outils institutionnels politique
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Secretariat General Directive 10.70 PDF, the graduate-studies PDF, and the UdeM privacy pages.
The official TI page links to the Secretariat directive and an authorized-tools list. The authorized-tools list redirects to Microsoft/ADFS login and was recorded as inaccessible.

methodsAttempted.query: official UdeM AI pages from cpu.umontreal.ca, integrite.umontreal.ca, ti.umontreal.ca, esp.umontreal.ca, secretariatgeneral.umontreal.ca
methodsAttempted.notes: Verified official pages and PDFs were fetched with Firecrawl and checked with direct HTTP headers. No Playwright escalation was needed for public pages.

artifacts.summary: Official UdeM discovery found a university Secretariat directive for administrative staff, a TI page on generative AI tool use and proscribed tools, institutional teaching-integrity guidance, a CPU teaching-resource landing page, and graduate-studies guidance for directed work, theses, doctoral essays, and dissertations. Evidence claims are limited to those source scopes. News/editorial and faculty toolbox pages were rejected for policy evidence, and the staff-only authorized-tools list was recorded as login-gated.

targets.sourceTitle: Directive pour l'utilisation de l'intelligence artificielle (IA) générative

stopConditions: Use only official UdeM-controlled public pages and PDFs as policy evidence.
Do not use news, search snippets, source titles, or faculty-only pages as claim evidence.
Record login-gated staff-only sources as inaccessible instead of attempting authentication.
Preserve French evidence snippets as canonical.

artifacts.claimText: UdeM's graduate-studies guidance says graduate students working with human-participant research data must not submit personal or identifying participant information, or information that could identify an individual or group, to generative AI tools.

artifacts.title: Université de Montréal staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Université de Montréal includes administrative-staff Directive 10.70 on generative AI, TI cybersecurity guidance naming proscribed tools, institutional teaching-integrity balises for course authorization and AI detection caution, and graduate-studies guidelines for transparent AI use and confidentiality in directed work, theses, doctoral essays, and dissertations.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to administrative staff, teaching/course guidance, and graduate directed work/thesis contexts where the source language is scoped that way.
The UdeM staff-only authorized generative AI tools list was login-gated and was not accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
